caption: A presentation slide for a "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" course, titled "Tutorial – 4," is displayed. The slide is authored by "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay," and features the NPTEL logo in the bottom-left corner. The initial frames focus on the section "Location of $h_1$ and Additional Work," featuring a mathematical formula for $\varepsilon$. As the sequence progresses, the formula for $\varepsilon$ is rearranged into an expression for $h_1$: $h_1 = \varepsilon(h_i - h_g) + h_g$. In the final frame, a data table is introduced containing columns for indices 1, 2, f, and g, with rows for pressure (p), temperature (T), enthalpy (h), and entropy (s). A final calculation is shown at the bottom of the slide: $h_1 = 0.9(462 - 230) + 230 = 438.8 \text{ J/g}$.
